Title: Reverse prenylation in plants by non-canonical aromatic prenyltransferases

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Hypericum sampsonii; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Hypericum sampsonii; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Non-canonical membrane-bound UbiA-type aromatic prenyltransferase; chloroplast transit peptide; aspartate-rich prenyltransferase motif
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Polyprenylated xanthone biosynthesis / xanthone prenylation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final prenylation step to hyperixanthone A branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; reverse prenylation itself is not indicated as the limiting step in the reconstructed yeast cascade, where gem-diprenylation is suggested to be more limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive natural product; hyperixanthone A is described as an inhibitor of multidrug-resistant Staphylococcus aureus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation and accumulation of reverse-prenylated hyperixanthone A from patulone; also catalyzes minor forward prenylation to allanxanthone C
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a dimethylallyl group from DMAPP to patulone, mainly as reverse C-4 aromatic prenylation to form hyperixanthone A, and secondarily as forward C-2 prenylation to form allanxanthone C
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-specific / strongly enriched in roots versus shoots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional in transiently transformed N. benthamiana leaves; native transcript enriched in roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root tissue context; transient agroinfiltration in N. benthamiana; feeding with patulone or 1,3,6,7-tetrahydroxyxanthone in yeast reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HsPT8px; HsPTpat (possible membrane interaction discussed for co-expressed upstream aPTs, not directly demonstrated for HsRPThxa)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript mining guided by root-specific expression; heterologous expression in yeast microsomes; enzyme assay with patulone and DMAPP; LC-ESI-HRMS/MS product verification; transient expression in N. benthamiana leaf disks; pathway reconstruction in S. cerevisiae; site-directed mutagenesis; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HsRPThxa was identified from root-enriched transcripts of H. sampsonii as the enzyme catalyzing the last prenylation step toward hyperixanthone A. 2. In vitro and in planta it converted patulone plus DMAPP mainly to hyperixanthone A via reverse C-4 prenylation, with allanxanthone C as a minor forward-prenylated side product. 3. Co-expression with HsPT8px and HsPTpat reconstituted the three-step prenylation cascade to hyperixanthone A in yeast. 4. Localization and truncation experiments indicated an N-terminal transit peptide important for chloroplast-envelope targeting and heterologous productivity. 5. Modeling and mutagenesis supported two substrate binding modes underlying reverse versus forward prenylation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Wild-type enzyme showed poor productive performance in yeast whole-cell reconstruction, likely due to mislocalization; truncation improved heterologous production but altered localization. Minor side-product formation (allanxanthone C) reduces strict product specificity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful biocatalyst for engineering reverse-prenylated xanthones and other valuable phenolics; can be stacked with HsPT8px and HsPTpat for pathway reconstruction; transit-peptide trimming, codon optimization, protein engineering, and alternative hosts may improve production
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL promoter in yeast; CaMV 35S promoter in N. benthamiana localization/plant expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: H. sampsonii root/shoot transcriptomics (de novo assembled transcriptomic data; accession not provided); metabolite profiling by LC-ESI-HRMS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HsRPThxa accepted DMAPP but not GPP. Apparent Km for patulone toward hyperixanthone A formation was 52.40 ± 4.16 µM and for DMAPP 102.21 ± 6.35 µM. Product ratio shifted toward more reverse prenylation in planta and in yeast resting cells than in microsomes. Truncated HsRPThxa lacking the first 24 aa showed higher substrate affinity and enabled detectable product formation in reconstructed yeast cells.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Hypericum perforatum; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Hypericum perforatum; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Non-canonical membrane-bound UbiA-type aromatic prenyltransferase; UbiA family
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Polyprenylated xanthone biosynthesis / xanthone prenylation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final prenylation step to hyperixanthone A branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive natural product; hyperixanthone A is described as an inhibitor of multidrug-resistant Staphylococcus aureus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of reverse-prenylated hyperixanthone A from patulone, with minor formation of allanxanthone C
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers dimethylallyl group from DMAPP to patulone, predominantly by reverse C-4 aromatic prenylation to hyperixanthone A and secondarily by forward C-2 prenylation to allanxanthone C
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily present in roots according to RNA-seq; root-upregulated and co-expressed with xanthone prenyltransferase homologs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional in transiently transformed N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root tissue context; transient agroinfiltration in N. benthamiana; substrate feeding with patulone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics and co-expression analysis; cloning from root cDNA; heterologous yeast microsome assays; LC-ESI-HRMS/MS product validation; transient expression in N. benthamiana leaf disks; kinetic characterization; molecular docking
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HpRPThxa was identified from H. perforatum root-enriched candidate transcripts homologous to the H. sampsonii enzyme. 2. It catalyzed the same dual reaction spectrum as HsRPThxa, producing mainly hyperixanthone A and minor allanxanthone C from patulone and DMAPP. 3. Activity was confirmed both in yeast microsomes and N. benthamiana leaf disks, where reverse prenylation dominated. 4. Docking supported two binding modes explaining reverse and forward prenylation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Forms minor off-target side product allanxanthone C; lower average activity than HsRPThxa in N. benthamiana leaf disks
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative reverse aromatic prenyltransferase for biocatalytic production and diversification of prenylated xanthones; useful comparative enzyme for engineering regioselectivity
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL promoter in yeast; plant transient expression promoter not explicitly stated for activity assay, likely constitutive vector-based expression; 35S used in localization construct context for HsRPThxa only
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: H. perforatum transcriptome from Medicinal Plant Genomics Resource and oneKP resources; RNA-seq expression heatmap data; metabolite profiling by LC-ESI-HRMS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Full-length ORF reconstructed from hpa_locus_53537 with oneKP/MPGR sequence information. It accepted DMAPP but not GPP. Apparent Km for patulone toward hyperixanthone A formation was 45.97 ± 4.43 µM and for DMAPP 259.99 ± 22.88 µM.
